steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank opens an introductory phrase (“______ in 1557”) that precedes the comma and the main clause “her painting can now be found at the Walters Art Museum.” An introductory verbal phrase must begin with a participle. Because the painting was completed in the past (1557), a past participle is required.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Will complete: future tense. A finite future-tense verb cannot begin an introductory participial phrase, and it contradicts the past date 1557. ✗
  • Choice B — Completed: past participle. “Completed in 1557” is a correct introductory participial phrase modifying her painting. ✓
  • Choice C — Completes: finite present-tense verb. A finite verb cannot begin an introductory participial phrase, and present tense contradicts 1557. ✗
  • Choice D — Was completed: finite past passive verb. This creates a second independent clause and a comma splice, not an introductory participial phrase. ✗
That is the correct answer.
